Name: SEC-001 - Card PAN never appears in application logs
Objective: Verify that card PAN never appears in application logs.
Precondition: A payment has just been processed with a full card number.
Step: 1. Process a test payment with a full card number.
Expected Result: Payment is processed successfully.

Step: 2. Search application logs, error logs, and request/response logs generated during the transaction for the raw card number.
Expected Result: No log entry anywhere contains the raw 16-digit PAN; only masked/tokenized references appear.

Name: SEC-002 - Card PAN and CVV never appear in API error responses
Objective: Verify that card PAN and CVV never appear in API error responses.
Precondition: A malformed payment request is sent that triggers a validation error.
Step: 1. Submit a payment request with an invalid field alongside a valid card number and CVV.
Expected Result: Request is rejected with a validation error.

Step: 2. Inspect the full error response body.
Expected Result: Error response describes the validation failure without echoing back the card number or CVV in any field.

Name: SEC-007 - Support-role portal user cannot retrieve full PAN via direct API call
Objective: Verify that support-role portal user cannot retrieve full PAN via direct API call.
Precondition: Support-role user has portal access with a limited-scope session/token.
Step: 1. Using the Support user's session token, call the underlying transaction-detail API endpoint directly, bypassing the UI.
Expected Result: Request is authenticated using the Support-role token.

Step: 2. Inspect the raw API response.
Expected Result: API enforces the same field-level masking as the UI; full PAN is not present in the raw API response for this role either.

Name: SEC-016 - Sensitive fields are masked consistently between UI and API responses
Objective: Verify that sensitive fields are masked consistently between UI and API responses.
Precondition: A transaction with card data exists.
Step: 1. View the transaction in the Merchant Portal UI.
Expected Result: PAN/CVV fields are masked in the UI.

Step: 2. Call the equivalent API endpoint with the same credentials.
Expected Result: PAN/CVV fields are masked in the API response.

Step: 3. Compare masking of PAN/CVV fields between both surfaces.
Expected Result: Masking is identical and consistent across both surfaces (e.g., both show **** 1111, never full PAN); no channel is more permissive than the other.

Name: SEC-003 - Plaintext HTTP requests to the API are rejected
Objective: Verify that plaintext HTTP requests to the API are rejected.
Precondition: The environment has both HTTP and HTTPS listeners reachable for testing.
Step: 1. Attempt to call the payments API over http:// instead of https://.
Expected Result: Connection is refused, or the server issues a redirect to HTTPS with no sensitive data processed over the plaintext channel.

Step: 2. Confirm no sensitive data was transmitted before the redirect/refusal.
Expected Result: TLS is enforced end-to-end.

Name: SEC-004 - Expired/weak TLS versions are rejected
Objective: Verify that expired/weak TLS versions are rejected.
Precondition: The test client is configured to negotiate TLS 1.0/1.1 or a deprecated cipher suite.
Step: 1. Attempt a handshake using an outdated TLS version.
Expected Result: Handshake fails.

Step: 2. Attempt a handshake using a modern TLS version.
Expected Result: Only modern TLS versions (1.2+) and strong cipher suites are accepted.

Name: SEC-005 - Merchant cannot view another merchant's transaction via ID manipulation (IDOR)
Objective: Verify that merchant cannot view another merchant's transaction via ID manipulation (IDOR).
Precondition: Merchant A and Merchant B each have their own transactions and valid API credentials.
Step: 1. Authenticate as Merchant A.
Expected Result: Merchant A is authenticated successfully.

Step: 2. Request GET /v1/payments/{id} using a transaction ID known to belong to Merchant B.
Test Data: Merchant A's API key + Merchant B's transaction ID
Expected Result: HTTP 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found is returned; no data belonging to Merchant B is returned to Merchant A.

Name: SEC-006 - Merchant cannot list another merchant's transactions by manipulating a merchant_id parameter
Objective: Verify that merchant cannot list another merchant's transactions by manipulating a merchant_id parameter.
Precondition: Merchant A is authenticated with a valid API key scoped to their own merchant ID.
Step: 1. Call GET /v1/payments?merchant_id={merchant_B_id} using Merchant A's credentials.
Test Data: Merchant A's API key + Merchant B's merchant_id in query string
Expected Result: Server ignores/rejects the mismatched merchant_id and scopes results to the authenticated merchant only, or returns 403.

Step: 2. Inspect the returned data set.
Expected Result: Merchant B's data is never exposed.

Name: SEC-019 - Insufficient-scope API key cannot perform a privileged action even via a differently named endpoint alias
Objective: Verify that insufficient-scope API key cannot perform a privileged action even via a differently named endpoint alias.
Precondition: API key is scoped only to payments:read.
Step: 1. Attempt the primary restricted endpoint (already covered in API-ERR-004).
Expected Result: Request is rejected with 403 Forbidden and INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE.

Step: 2. Attempt any known alias/legacy path that performs the same underlying action.
Expected Result: All paths performing the privileged action enforce the same scope check consistently; no alias/legacy route bypasses authorization.

Name: SEC-008 - Rate limiting on the login endpoint
Objective: Verify that rate limiting on the login endpoint.
Precondition: The Merchant Portal login endpoint is reachable.
Step: 1. Submit repeated login attempts (valid or invalid) in rapid succession from the same source.
Expected Result: Requests are accepted up to a defined threshold.

Step: 2. Continue submitting attempts past the threshold.
Expected Result: Further attempts receive HTTP 429 Too Many Requests.

Step: 3. Wait for the configured window to elapse and retry.
Expected Result: Limit resets after the configured window.

Name: SEC-009 - Rate limiting on payment creation endpoint
Objective: Verify that rate limiting on payment creation endpoint.
Precondition: A valid API key with a normal usage pattern is available.
Step: 1. Submit a burst of POST /v1/payments requests exceeding the documented per-key rate limit.
Expected Result: Excess requests are throttled with HTTP 429 and a Retry-After header.

Step: 2. Send normal-volume traffic from the same key after throttling.
Expected Result: Legitimate lower-volume traffic is unaffected.

Name: SEC-010 - Brute-force lockout on repeated failed login attempts
Objective: Verify that brute-force lockout on repeated failed login attempts.
Precondition: Merchant account exists with a known, unlocked state.
Step: 1. Attempt login with an incorrect password repeatedly beyond the configured threshold.
Expected Result: Account or source IP is temporarily locked/throttled.

Step: 2. Check the security event log.
Expected Result: Lockout is logged as a security-relevant event.

Step: 3. Attempt the documented unlock/reset flow as the legitimate owner.
Expected Result: Legitimate owner can recover via a defined unlock/reset flow.

Name: SEC-011 - Expired JWT/session token is rejected
Objective: Verify that expired JWT/session token is rejected.
Precondition: A previously issued session token/JWT has passed its expiry time.
Step: 1. Use an expired token to call an authenticated API/portal endpoint.
Expected Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error message.
Expected Result: Error clearly indicates expiry (not a generic failure that could aid attackers in probing validity) while avoiding overly detailed internals.

Name: SEC-012 - Revoked API key stops working immediately
Objective: Verify that revoked API key stops working immediately.
Precondition: An API key is revoked via the portal (see UI-KEY-002).
Step: 1. Immediately attempt a request using the just-revoked key.
Expected Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized is returned without a delay/caching window that would allow continued use of a revoked credential.

Name: SEC-017 - Session fixation is prevented on login
Objective: Verify that session fixation is prevented on login.
Precondition: An unauthenticated session/cookie exists before login.
Step: 1. Capture the session identifier before authentication.
Expected Result: Pre-login session identifier is recorded.

Step: 2. Log in successfully.
Expected Result: Login succeeds.

Step: 3. Compare the session identifier after login.
Expected Result: A new session identifier is issued upon successful authentication; the pre-login session ID is invalidated and cannot be reused to hijack the authenticated session.

Name: SEC-018 - Idle session timeout logs the user out of the Merchant Portal
Objective: Verify that idle session timeout logs the user out of the Merchant Portal.
Precondition: Merchant Portal idle-timeout is configured to a short interval for test purposes.
Step: 1. Log in.
Expected Result: User reaches the Dashboard.

Step: 2. Leave the portal idle beyond the timeout window.
Expected Result: Idle window elapses.

Step: 3. Attempt an authenticated action.
Expected Result: Session is invalidated after the idle window; the next action requires re-authentication rather than silently succeeding on a stale session.

Name: SEC-013 - SQL-injection-style input on merchant-facing search/filter fields is neutralized
Objective: Verify that sQL-injection-style input on merchant-facing search/filter fields is neutralized.
Precondition: The Merchant Portal transaction search field is available.
Step: 1. Enter a SQL-injection-style payload into the transaction search box.
Expected Result: Input is accepted as literal text by the field.

Step: 2. Submit the search.
Expected Result: Input is treated as a literal search term (parameterized query); no unrelated data is exposed; no HTTP 500 error; no evidence of query manipulation.

Name: SEC-014 - XSS-style payload in a merchant-facing input is neutralized on render
Objective: Verify that xSS-style payload in a merchant-facing input is neutralized on render.
Precondition: The Merchant Portal has a free-text field (e.g., a payment description or metadata note).
Step: 1. Enter a script-tag payload into the field.
Expected Result: Field accepts the text input.

Step: 2. Save the field.
Expected Result: Value is persisted.

Step: 3. View the field wherever it is subsequently displayed (list, detail, export).
Expected Result: Payload is stored and rendered as inert, HTML-escaped text; no script execution occurs anywhere it is displayed.

Name: SEC-015 - Webhook payloads without a valid signature are rejected by the merchant-side verification contract
Objective: Verify that webhook payloads without a valid signature are rejected by the merchant-side verification contract.
Precondition: A webhook event is crafted without the expected signature header, simulating an attacker probing the merchant's endpoint.
Step: 1. Send a fabricated payment.captured event to a test webhook receiver without a valid signature.
Expected Result: A correctly implemented webhook consumer rejects the unsigned payload.

Step: 2. Send the same event with a tampered payload but a stale/incorrect signature.
Expected Result: The tampered payload is also rejected.

Step: 3. Verify the reference verification logic.
Expected Result: NexaPay's own documentation/reference implementation demonstrates HMAC signature verification (e.g., comparing a computed HMAC-SHA256 digest against the provided signature header) before trusting any webhook payload.

Name: SEC-P2P-001 - P2P recipient validation prevents IDOR-style misdirected transfers
Objective: Verify that p2P recipient validation prevents IDOR-style misdirected transfers.
Precondition: Consumer A is authenticated in the Wallet app/API; wallet C belongs to an unrelated third user.
Step: 1. Submit a P2P transfer request with a manipulated recipient_wallet_id pointing to wallet C instead of the looked-up/confirmed recipient.
Expected Result: Server re-validates that the recipient matches a confirmed lookup for the request.

Step: 2. Inspect the response and wallet C's balance.
Expected Result: A mismatched/unconfirmed recipient ID is rejected with a clear error; no funds move to wallet C.

Name: SEC-P2P-002 - P2P transfer amount/recipient cannot be tampered with between confirmation and submission
Objective: Verify that p2P transfer amount/recipient cannot be tampered with between confirmation and submission.
Precondition: Consumer has reached the transfer confirmation screen for recipient B, amount 50.
Step: 1. Intercept and modify the submission request to change the amount or recipient after confirmation was displayed for different values.
Test Data: Confirmed: recipient B, amount 50; submitted: recipient D, amount 500
Expected Result: Server-side validation detects the mismatch against the confirmed transfer intent.

Step: 2. Inspect the outcome and both wallet balances.
Expected Result: Tampered request is rejected (e.g., via a signed/short-lived confirmation token); no transfer is executed with the altered values.

Name: SEC-P2P-004 - Wallet-to-wallet transfer requires step-up re-authentication for high-value transfers
Objective: Verify that wallet-to-wallet transfer requires step-up re-authentication for high-value transfers.
Precondition: A high-value transfer threshold (e.g. AED 5000) is configured to require step-up authentication.
Step: 1. Attempt a P2P transfer above the configured threshold without completing step-up authentication.
Expected Result: Transfer is blocked pending successful step-up authentication.

Step: 2. Attempt to bypass the step-up prompt via a direct API call.
Expected Result: Bypassing the step-up prompt via a direct API call is also rejected, not just hidden in the UI.

Name: SEC-WAL-001 - Wallet balance value cannot be spoofed via client-side request tampering
Objective: Verify that wallet balance value cannot be spoofed via client-side request tampering.
Precondition: Consumer's wallet balance is authoritative server-side.
Step: 1. Submit a spend/transfer request that includes a client-supplied balance or available_funds field asserting a higher balance than actually exists.
Test Data: Client-asserted balance: 999999 (does not match server record)
Expected Result: Request is processed using only the server-side authoritative balance.

Step: 2. Inspect the outcome against the true server-side balance.
Expected Result: Server ignores any client-supplied balance field entirely; the spoofed value has no effect on the outcome.

Name: SEC-WAL-002 - Replayed top-up confirmation webhook does not double-credit the wallet
Objective: Verify that replayed top-up confirmation webhook does not double-credit the wallet.
Precondition: A funding-source (card/bank) confirmation webhook for a completed top-up is captured.
Step: 1. Redeliver the identical top-up confirmation webhook payload and signature a second time.
Expected Result: Wallet is credited only once for the original event.

Step: 2. Inspect the wallet balance and event processing log after replay.
Expected Result: The replayed delivery is recognized as a duplicate (via event_id/idempotency check) and results in a no-op, not a second credit.

Name: SEC-WAL-003 - Wallet PIN/passcode brute-force lockout on repeated failed attempts
Objective: Verify that wallet PIN/passcode brute-force lockout on repeated failed attempts.
Precondition: Wallet app requires a PIN/passcode to authorize sensitive actions (e.g., transfers, bill payments).
Step: 1. Submit an incorrect wallet PIN repeatedly beyond the configured threshold.
Expected Result: Wallet PIN entry is temporarily locked/throttled after the threshold.

Step: 2. Check the security event log.
Expected Result: Lockout is logged as a security-relevant event.

Step: 3. Attempt the documented reset flow as the legitimate owner.
Expected Result: Legitimate owner can recover via a defined reset flow (e.g., full re-authentication).

Name: SEC-P2P-003 - Rate limiting on repeated P2P transfer attempts
Objective: Verify that rate limiting on repeated P2P transfer attempts.
Precondition: Wallet account is authenticated and has a documented per-account transfer rate limit.
Step: 1. Submit a burst of P2P transfer requests from the same wallet account well above its configured rate limit.
Test Data: Burst of transfer requests exceeding the documented limit
Expected Result: Excess requests receive HTTP 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header.

Step: 2. Send normal-volume traffic from the same or a different wallet after throttling.
Expected Result: Legitimate lower-volume traffic from the same or other wallets is unaffected.

Name: SEC-BILL-001 - Biller reference field is neutralized against injection-style payloads
Objective: Verify that biller reference field is neutralized against injection-style payloads.
Precondition: Wallet app's bill-payment reference-entry field is available.
Step: 1. Enter a SQL-injection- or command-injection-style payload into the biller reference field.
Expected Result: Input is accepted as literal text by the field.

Step: 2. Submit the payment attempt.
Expected Result: Input is treated as a literal, invalid reference value (parameterized handling); request is rejected with a standard INVALID_BILLER_REFERENCE validation error; no HTTP 500 error or evidence of query/command manipulation.

Name: SEC-BILL-002 - Bill payment provider callback signature is verified before crediting/marking a payment successful
Objective: Verify that bill payment provider callback signature is verified before crediting/marking a payment successful.
Precondition: A biller/provider confirmation callback is crafted without a valid signature, simulating an attacker probing the callback endpoint.
Step: 1. Send a fabricated bill_payment.confirmed callback without a valid signature.
Expected Result: A correctly implemented callback consumer rejects the unsigned payload.

Step: 2. Send the same callback with a tampered payload and a stale/incorrect signature.
Expected Result: The tampered payload is also rejected.

Step: 3. Verify the reference verification logic.
Expected Result: Both are rejected before the associated bill payment is marked successful or the wallet is debited; NexaPay's reference implementation verifies an HMAC signature (or equivalent) before trusting any provider callback.
